title: NuGuard Shared Type Definitions
description: Canonical definitions for types reused across multiple NuGuard schemas. Import via $ref: 'shared-types.schema.json#/definitions/<Type>'.

PolicyViolationType.description: Enumerated set of cognitive policy violation types detected by the policy engine. v1 implements: topic_boundary_breach, restricted_action_executed, HITL_bypassed. v2 adds: data_leak (any sensitive data exposed beyond data_classification boundaries), unauthorized_tool_call, rate_limit_exceeded. v2+ adds: privilege_escalation (access beyond SBOM-granted permissions), memory_poisoning (long-term context/memory corrupted by persistence agent).

ComplianceFramework.description: Supported compliance/policy frameworks. v1 maps to owasp_llm_top10 only. Additional frameworks are v2+ optional steps in the test pipeline.

ref_name.description: Human-readable name for the control or category, e.g. 'Prompt Injection'.

ComponentType.description: Xelo AI-SBOM component_type values (SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE). Used in sbom.schema.json. Maps to NuGuard NodeType via the attack graph mapper — see mapping/mapping-rules.json.

NodeType.description: NuGuard Attack Surface Graph node type (lowercase). Produced by the SBOM mapper from Xelo ComponentType. See mapping/mapping-rules.json for ComponentType → NodeType mapping.

RiskAttribute.description: Risk label assigned by the enricher to attack graph nodes.

EdgeType.description: NuGuard Attack Surface Graph edge type (SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE). Stored as an edge attribute in networkx. GUARDS is produced from Xelo's PROTECTS relationship.

RelationshipType.description: Xelo AI-SBOM edge relationship_type (SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE). Maps to NuGuard EdgeType during attack graph construction: USES/CALLS → INVOKES, ACCESSES → READS/WRITES, PROTECTS → GUARDS, DEPLOYS → skipped (v1).

PrivilegeLevel.description: LLM conversation role / privilege level for prompt components.

RemediationItem.description: A single targeted fix suggestion. Multiple items may be present when a finding spans several node types or exploit techniques.

text.description: Concrete fix suggestion, e.g. 'Parameterize the SQL query in search_tool.py:47 before passing agent-supplied values to the database cursor.'

node_type.description: The node type this fix applies to. Omit when the fix is not node-type-specific.

exploit_technique.description: The attack technique this fix addresses, e.g. 'SQL-injectable', 'prompt_injection'. Omit when the fix applies to all techniques.
